skill.name: vue-pinia-best-practices
skill.description: Guidance for applying Pinia state management patterns, fixing common setup errors, and preserving Vue reactivity.
skill.summary: Apply Pinia best practices for Vue stores, reactivity, SSR, URL state, and store setup.
skill.author: github.com/vuejs-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged dynamic imports, shell-like backticks, fetch calls, hardcoded documentation URLs, localStorage mentions, and weak-crypto keyword matches. Manual review found these patterns inside Markdown tutorials and Vue or JavaScript examples, not executable skill code. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious command execution intent was found.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Dismissed as Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The external command, weak-crypto, system reconnaissance, and script detections occur in Markdown prose, checklists, links, or fenced Vue and JavaScript examples. They are not runnable skill installation scripts and do not create command execution risk by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown guidance and code-fenced tutorial material. I found no executable script file or install hook that would run these snippets automatically.

low_findings.title: Educational Snippets Mention Tokens and Persistence
low_findings.description: Some examples demonstrate Authorization headers, authToken state, and persisted store behavior. This is legitimate Pinia teaching content, but users should avoid persisting secrets or exposing sensitive state without project-specific controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples are clearly instructional and use relative API paths, not external exfiltration endpoints. The residual concern is misuse by users who copy persistence patterns for sensitive tokens.

content.value_statement: Vue applications often fail because store setup, destructuring, and URL state rules are easy to miss. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused Pinia guidance for reliable stores and predictable reactivity.
actual_capabilities: Diagnoses the common getActivePinia startup error and plugin ordering issues.
Explains when to call stores inside setup functions, composables, router guards, or initialized code paths.
Shows how storeToRefs preserves reactivity when destructuring Pinia state and getters.
Guides setup stores to return all state needed for SSR, DevTools, and plugins.
Compares URL query state, Pinia stores, and hand-rolled reactive stores for Vue applications.
Provides migration guidance from simple reactive stores or Vuex patterns toward Pinia.
limitations: It provides written guidance and examples, not an automated code migration tool.
It focuses on Vue 3 and Pinia patterns, not every Vue state library.
It does not verify runtime behavior unless the user asks the agent to inspect or test a project.
Security choices for token storage and persistence still require project-specific review.

use_cases.title: Fix Pinia Startup Failures
use_cases.description: Use the skill to identify why a store is called before Pinia is installed and adjust app or router setup order.

use_cases.title: Review Store Reactivity Bugs
use_cases.description: Use the skill to find direct destructuring, missing storeToRefs usage, and method binding mistakes in Vue components.

use_cases.title: Design Scalable State Patterns
use_cases.description: Use the skill to decide when data belongs in Pinia, URL query parameters, component state, or a simpler reactive store.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Store Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Vue app setup for Pinia ordering problems. Explain why getActivePinia errors happen and show the smallest safe fix.

prompt_templates.title: Find Reactivity Mistakes
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect this component for Pinia destructuring or method binding issues. Tell me what should use storeToRefs and what can be destructured directly.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Setup Store Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Pinia setup store for SSR, DevTools, and plugin compatibility. Identify any state that should be returned or renamed.

prompt_templates.title: Plan State Ownership
prompt_templates.prompt: Map this feature state across Pinia, URL query parameters, and local component state. Explain the tradeoffs for refresh, sharing, privacy, and debugging.

output_examples.input: A Vue app crashes with getActivePinia during startup.
output_examples.output: The response identifies early store access, checks plugin order, and recommends installing Pinia before router guards that use stores.

output_examples.input: A component destructures name and isLoggedIn from a Pinia store and the UI stops updating.
output_examples.output: The response explains that state and getters need storeToRefs, while actions can remain direct function references.

output_examples.input: A products page stores filters only in Pinia and loses them on refresh.
output_examples.output: The response recommends URL query parameters for shareable filters and keeps private or non-shareable state in the store.

best_practices: Install Pinia before plugins, router guards, or modules that call stores.
Use storeToRefs for state and getters, and destructure actions directly.
Keep shareable filters in the URL and reserve stores for application state.
anti_patterns: Calling useStore at module scope before the Vue app has installed Pinia.
Directly destructuring Pinia state or getters and expecting reactive updates.
Persisting every store field without reviewing privacy, security, and hydration needs.

faq.question: What Vue version is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is written for Vue 3 applications that use Pinia for state management.

faq.question: Can it help with getActivePinia errors?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains store initialization timing, plugin order, router guard access, and safe store call locations.

faq.question: Does it replace Pinia documentation?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes common gotchas and points agents toward practical review patterns.

faq.question: Can it migrate Vuex code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can guide migration decisions, but code changes depend on the project structure.

faq.question: Does it cover SSR concerns?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains why setup stores should return all relevant state for hydration, DevTools, and plugins.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with private application code?
faq.answer: The skill is documentation-only, but users should still review generated advice before applying persistence or token handling patterns.
